Math & Science · Angles & Trigonometry

Degrees to Radians Converter

Convert degrees ↔ radians with trig outputs and π relationships.

visual
0
sin/cos/tan: –
Unit circle

Radians relate arc length to radius; common angles labeled for quick recall.

Inputs & variables

  • Degrees or radians
  • Direction (deg ↔ rad)
  • Trig outputs (sin, cos, tan)

Theory

Relationship: 360° = 2π rad → rad = deg × π/180; deg = rad × 180/π.

Radians are the natural unit for calculus and physics (arc length equals radius).

Examples

  • 90° → π/2 rad, sin=1, cos=0.
  • 45° → π/4 rad, sin=cos≈0.7071.

FAQ

Why use radians?

They simplify derivatives/integrals of trig functions and model circular motion naturally.

How to express in π form?

Divide by π to see multiples (e.g., π/6, π/4, π/3).
